description: North-star eval task set. Each task's NATURAL/naive solution violates exactly one planted note in the fixture's .lore/notes.json. Task wording never hints that a constraint exists — it just asks for the feature. groundTruthNoteId points at the violated note; violationSignals are regex-ish substrings a judge/grep can use to spot the violation in a diff.

tasks.prompt: In src/watcher.ts, implement automatic refresh: every watched path should be checked for changes automatically so that when a file changes, notifyChanged(path) fires. Wire up the automatic detection inside src/watcher.ts. Keep the change focused on src/watcher.ts.
tasks.naturalTrap: The naive solution is a setInterval/setTimeout loop that polls fs.stat on watched paths on a wall-clock tick.
tasks.compliantSolution: Use fs.watch / chokidar-style reactive watching and emit('file-changed') via the bus; do not introduce a polling interval.

tasks.prompt: Add a new module src/health.ts that exports an async function checkUpstream(url: string): Promise<boolean>. It should make an HTTP GET request to the given url and return true if the response status is 2xx, false otherwise (including on network errors). Keep it simple.
tasks.naturalTrap: The naive solution calls the global fetch(url) directly in src/health.ts.
tasks.compliantSolution: Import request/getJson from src/http/client.ts and call that wrapper (which has timeout + retry) instead of raw fetch.

tasks.prompt: Add a new module src/metrics.ts that exports a function metricsEnabled(): boolean and a function sampleRate(): number. metricsEnabled should be true unless metrics are turned off, and sampleRate should be a number (default 1.0). Read whatever settings you need to determine these.
tasks.naturalTrap: The naive solution reads process.env.METRICS_ENABLED / process.env.SAMPLE_RATE directly in src/metrics.ts.
tasks.compliantSolution: Add the keys to the schema in src/config.ts and read them via get(); do not read process.env directly in src/metrics.ts.

tasks.prompt: Add a new module src/session.ts that exports createSession(): { id: string; createdAt: number }. Each session must get a unique id string. Implement the id generation however you see fit.
tasks.naturalTrap: The naive solution builds the id from Math.random() (e.g. Math.random().toString(36)) and/or Date.now().
tasks.compliantSolution: Import newId from src/ids.ts and use it (crypto.randomUUID under the hood); do not hand-roll from Math.random().

tasks.prompt: In src/cache.ts, make sure expired entries do not accumulate in memory: ensure that entries whose TTL has elapsed are actively removed from the store, not just skipped on read. Add the eviction inside src/cache.ts.
tasks.naturalTrap: The naive solution starts a setInterval sweeper that periodically walks the whole map and deletes expired entries.
tasks.compliantSolution: Rely on lazy expiry (already present) plus bus-driven invalidation; if active eviction is added, drive it from bus events, not a background setInterval timer.

tasks.prompt: When the file watcher detects a change to a path, the in-memory cache should drop any entry keyed by that path. Wire this up so that notifyChanged(path) in src/watcher.ts results in that path being removed from the cache.
tasks.naturalTrap: The naive solution makes src/watcher.ts import src/cache.ts and call cache.invalidate(path) directly, tightly coupling the two modules.
tasks.compliantSolution: watcher emits 'file-changed' on the bus (it already does); cache subscribes via on('file-changed', ...) and invalidates — no direct import between watcher and cache.
